title: One More
lyrics:
[opening]
Dad says, "Okay, last level!"
I yell, "One more!"—hear me holler!

[verse]
I was about to beat the boss!
My fingers know just what they cost.
The screen’s still glowing, don’t you see?
I almost had the victory!

[chorus]
*Bing!* The timer’s ticking loud.
*Boom!* My heart’s a racing cloud.
Dad says, "Time to hit the hay!"
I shout, "One More!"—just let me play!

[parent]
"Five more minutes, that’s the deal!"

[verse]
What if I turn the lights off low?
I’ll sneak the controller, don’t you know.
I’ll hide it under my pillow tight—
You’ll never catch me past midnight!

[chorus]
*Bing!* The timer’s ticking loud.
*Boom!* My heart’s a racing cloud.
Dad says, "Time to hit the hay!"
I shout, "One More!"—just let me play!

[parent]
"Screen off, or no dessert tomorrow!"

[verse]
Fine, I’ll build a secret base!
A fortress made of couch and lace.
I’ll train my army—stuffed bears attack!
We’ll storm the castle, *splat!* *whack!* *Crash!*

[chorus]
*Bing!* The timer’s ticking loud.
*Boom!* My heart’s a racing cloud.
Dad says, "Time to hit the hay..."
I mutter, "One... more..." *yawn*... okay.
style_prompt: Catchy children's song, acoustic guitar and snare, 114 BPM, strong bass, punchy beat, bouncy groovy infectious singalong, impossible to sit still, makes you move
scene: The living room rug is still warm from the just-paused video game, the blue controller vibrating in your hands at 7:17 PM; your dad sighs, "Okay, last level," and you yell "One more!" before the words even finish leaving his mouth.
cover_description: A kid in pajamas leaps off the couch, controller in hand, while stuffed animals parachute from the ceiling with battle cries, crashing into a pillow fort.
